title: Lesson 10 — Nerve

words.definition: showing a willingness to take bold or surprising risks
words.example: An audacious plan to cross the desert on foot.
Intrepid explorers mapped the frozen coast.
Dauntless in the storm, she held the wheel all night.

words.definition: excessive boldness or nerve; reckless audacity
words.example: He had the temerity to correct the professor mid-lecture.

words.definition: showing or suffering from nervousness and fear
words.example: A timorous knock, barely audible, came at the door.
A craven retreat left the others dangerously exposed.

words.definition: showing a lack of courage or determination; timid
words.example: His pusillanimous silence let the lie go unchallenged.

words.definition: a feeling of fear or nervous anxiety about what may happen
words.example: She opened the exam results with real trepidation.
A brazen theft carried out in broad daylight.
Swimming out that far in the current was foolhardy.

words.definition: great courage in the face of danger
words.example: He received a medal for valor under fire.

words.definition: to crouch or shrink back in fear
words.example: The dog cowered under the table at the thunder.

story: The captain's [[audacious|audacious]] plan called for a night crossing, and only the most [[intrepid|intrepid]] of the crew volunteered. Reyes was [[dauntless|dauntless]]; young Oporto had the [[temerity|temerity]] to argue the route with her to her face. The [[timorous|timorous]] cook stayed below, and one [[craven|craven]] deckhand simply hid. "Better [[pusillanimous|pusillanimous]] than drowned," he muttered. The rest set out with quiet [[trepidation|trepidation]]. A [[brazen|brazen]], almost [[foolhardy|foolhardy]] dash across the reef nearly capsized them — until an act of pure [[valor|valor]] at the tiller saved the boat, while the coward continued to [[cower|cower]] in the hold.
